title: Recipes for some analyses modifications
text:
Code
    
    

    
        Where we present some piece of codes to modify the bioinformatic pipeline
    
    
        Author: Adrien Taudière
    
    
        
      Date: June 22, 2026
See also Easy16S for inspiration in bash.

title: Recipes for some analyses modifications
section: Change the parameter to merged forward and reverse sequences
text:
Change the parameter to merged forward and reverse sequences

Play with option min Overlap (default to 12) and maxMismach (default to 0). For example, replace the merged_seq targets with the following code to allow 1 mismatch and a minimum overlap of 8.


tar_target(
    merged_seq,
    mergePairs(
      dadaF = ddF,
      dadaR = ddR,
      derepF = derep_fs,
      derepR = derep_rs,
      minOverlap = 8,
      maxMismatch = 1)
    ),
    format = "qs"
  )
)

title: Recipes for some analyses modifications
text:
Forward only pipeline

Replace all the “paired end” area with the following code


  ##> Remove primers
  tar_target(
    cutadapt,
    cutadapt_remove_primers(
      path_to_fastq = here("data/data_raw/rawseq/"),
      primer_fw = fw_primer_sequences,
      folder_output = here("data/data_intermediate/seq_wo_primers/"),
      args_before_cutadapt = "source ~/miniforge3/etc/profile.d/conda.sh && conda activate cutadaptenv && "
    )
  ),
  tar_target(data_raw, {
    cutadapt
    list_fastq_files(path = here::here("data/data_intermediate/seq_wo_primers/"),
                     paired_end = FALSE)
  }),

  ##> Classical dada2 pipeline
  tar_target(data_fnfs, data_raw$fnfs),
  ### Pre-filtered data with low stringency
  tar_target(
    filtered,
    filter_trim(
      output_fw = paste(
        getwd(),
        here("/data/data_intermediate/filterAndTrim_fwd"),
        sep = ""
      ),
      rev = data_fnrs,
      multithread = n_threads,
      compress = TRUE
    )
  ),

  ### Dereplicate fastq files
  tar_target(derep_fs, derepFastq(filtered[[1]]), format = "qs"),
  tar_target(derep_rs, derepFastq(filtered[[2]]), format = "qs"),
  ### Learns the error rates
  tar_target(err_fs, learnErrors(derep_fs, multithread = 4), format = "qs"),
  tar_target(err_rs, learnErrors(derep_rs, multithread = 4), format = "qs"),
  ### Make amplicon sequence variants
  tar_target(ddF, dada(derep_fs, err_fs, multithread = 4), format = "qs"),
  tar_target(ddR, dada(derep_rs, err_rs, multithread = 4), format = "qs"),
  ### Build a a table of ASV x Samples
  tar_target(seq_tab, makeSequenceTable(ddF)),

title: Recipes for some analyses modifications
section: Add a second taxonomic assignation using a different database or algorithm
text:
Add a second taxonomic assignation using a different database or algorithm

Add a new database file (fasta) in data/data_raw/refseq
Copy and complete with good names the two targets below
Rename the targets by using the new name (e.g. data_phyloseq_newDB) instead of data_phyloseq in the subsequent targets


[...]
tar_target(
    name = file_refseq_taxo2,
    command = "data/data_raw/refseq/XXX",
    format = "file"
)

[...]

tar_target(
  data_phyloseq_newDB,
  add_new_taxonomy_pq(
    data_phyloseq,
    file_refseq_taxo2,
    suffix = "PR2",
    taxLevels = c(
      "Kingdom",
      "Supergroup",
      "Division",
      "Subdivision",
      "Class",
      "Order",
      "Family",
      "Genus",
      "Species"
    )
  )
)

title: Recipes for some analyses modifications
text:
Filter by % sequence (whith blast)

tar_target(d_blast,
    filter_taxa_blast(
      data_phyloseq,
      fasta_for_db = paste0(here::here(), "/", file_refseq_taxo),
      nproc = 4
    )
)

title: Recipes for some analyses modifications
text:
Add funguild informations for Fungi

tar_target(d_funguild, 
  MiscMetabar::add_funguild_info(data_phyloseq)
)

section: Remove n nucleotides at the left or right end
text:
Remove n nucleotides at the left or right end
You may want to use the trimLeft and/or trimRight arguments of filter_trim function.

filter_trim(
#      [...],
      trimLeft = 1,
      trimRight = 1
)

title: Bioinformatics pipeline summary
text:
Timeline and cpu usage

Code
log_data <- autometric::log_read(
  here::here("data/data_final/autometric_log.txt"),
  units_time = "hours",
  units_memory = "gigabytes"
)

log_data |>
  filter(!grepl("conclude:", phase)) |>
  filter(!grepl("prepare:", phase)) |>
  filter(!grepl("__DEFAULT__", phase)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = time, y = reorder(phase, desc(time)), color = resident)) +
  geom_line(aes(linewidth = cpu)) +
  scale_color_viridis_b("Memory (Gb)", end = 0.9, direction = -1) +
  theme_idest() +
  xlab("Time (in hours)") +
  ylab("")

title: Bioinformatics pipeline summary
text:
Timeline and cpu usage

Code
log_data_mcrA <- autometric::log_read(
  here::here("data/data_final/autometric_log_mcrA.txt"),
  units_time = "hours",
  units_memory = "gigabytes"
)

log_data_mcrA |>
  filter(!grepl("conclude:", phase)) |>
  filter(!grepl("prepare:", phase)) |>
  filter(!grepl("__DEFAULT__", phase)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = time, y = reorder(phase, desc(time)), color = resident)) +
  geom_line(aes(linewidth = cpu)) +
  scale_color_viridis_b("Memory (Gb)", end = 0.9, direction = -1) +
  theme_idest() +
  xlab("Time (in hours)") +
  ylab("")

title: Bioinformatics pipeline summary
text:
Carbon footprint

Code
res_ga_pmoA <- greenAlgoR::ga_targets(store = here::here("_targets_pmoA/"), PSF=2)
print(paste0(round(res_ga_pmoA$carbon_footprint_total_gCO2, 0), " gCO2 equiv. emitted for the whole pipeline"))

#> [1] "49 gCO2 equiv. emitted for the whole pipeline"

title: Bioinformatics pipeline summary
text:
Timeline and cpu usage

Code
log_data_pmoA <- autometric::log_read(
  here::here("data/data_final/autometric_log_pmoA.txt"),
  units_time = "hours",
  units_memory = "gigabytes"
)

log_data_pmoA |>
  filter(!grepl("conclude:", phase)) |>
  filter(!grepl("prepare:", phase)) |>
  filter(!grepl("__DEFAULT__", phase)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = time, y = reorder(phase, desc(time)), color = resident)) +
  geom_line(aes(linewidth = cpu)) +
  scale_color_viridis_b("Memory (Gb)", end = 0.9, direction = -1) +
  theme_idest() +
  xlab("Time (in hours)") +
  ylab("")

  mcrA_rev = "CGTTCATBGCGTAGTTVGGRTAGT",

check_primers_in_refseq <- function(primers, ps_list) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(names(ps_list), function(ps_name) {
    refseq <- ps_list[[ps_name]]@refseq
    do.call(rbind, lapply(names(primers), function(p_name) {
      p <- Biostrings::DNAString(primers[[p_name]])
      rc_p <- Biostrings::reverseComplement(p)
      fwd <- Biostrings::vcountPattern(p, refseq, fixed = FALSE) > 0
      rev <- Biostrings::vcountPattern(rc_p, refseq, fixed = FALSE) > 0
      data.frame(
        phyloseq = ps_name,
        primer = p_name,
        sequence = primers[[p_name]],
        n_fwd = sum(fwd),
        n_rev = sum(rev),
        n_any = sum(fwd | rev)
      )
    }))
  }))
}

all_primers <- c(
  archaea_16S_fwd = "CCCTACGGGGTGCASCAG",
  archaea_16S_rev = "GGACTACVSGGGTATCTAAT",
  mcrA_fwd        = "GGTGGTGTMGGDTTCACMCARTA",
  mcrA_rev        = "CGTTCATBGCGTAGTTVGGRTAGT",
  pmoA_fwd        = "GGNGACTGGGACTTCTGG",
  pmoA_rev        = "CCGGMGCAACGTCYTTACC"
)

#' Find taxa whose refseq contains any primer sequence
#'
#' For each taxon in `ps`, checks whether the representative sequence in
#' `@refseq` contains any primer from `primers` (forward or reverse complement),
#' using IUPAC-aware pattern matching (`fixed = FALSE`).
#'
#' @param ps A phyloseq object with a populated `@refseq` slot.
#' @param primers Named character vector of primer sequences (IUPAC codes
#'   allowed, e.g. M, R, Y, N).
#'
#' @return A data.frame with columns `taxon`, `matched_primers` (comma-separated
#'   names of all primers that matched), and `n_reads` (total reads across
#'   samples). Returns `NULL` if no contaminated taxa are found.
find_taxa_with_primers <- function(ps, primers) {
  refseq <- ps@refseq
  match_mat <- vapply(names(primers), function(p_name) {
    p <- Biostrings::DNAString(primers[[p_name]])
    rc_p <- Biostrings::reverseComplement(p)
    fwd <- Biostrings::vcountPattern(p, refseq, fixed = FALSE) > 0
    rev <- Biostrings::vcountPattern(rc_p, refseq, fixed = FALSE) > 0
    fwd | rev
  }, logical(length(refseq)))

  any_match <- rowSums(match_mat) > 0
  if (!any(any_match)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  bad_idx <- which(any_match)
  data.frame(
    taxon = names(refseq)[bad_idx],
    matched_primers = apply(match_mat[bad_idx, , drop = FALSE], 1, \(row) paste(names(primers)[row], collapse = ", ")),
    n_reads = phyloseq::taxa_sums(ps)[bad_idx],
    row.names = NULL
  )
}

bad_taxa <- list(
  "16S"  = find_taxa_with_primers(d_16S, all_primers),
  "pmoA" = find_taxa_with_primers(d_pmoA, all_primers),
  "mcrA" = find_taxa_with_primers(d_mcrA, all_primers)
)

for (ps_name in names(bad_taxa)) {
  df <- bad_taxa[[ps_name]]
  if (is.null(df) || nrow(df) == 0) {
    message(ps_name, ": no taxa with primer contamination.")
  } else {
    print(kable(
      df[order(-df$n_reads), ],
      caption = sprintf("%s — %d taxa containing a primer sequence (%d total reads)", ps_name, nrow(df), sum(df$n_reads))
    ))
  }
}

filter_primer_taxa <- function(ps, primers) {
  bad <- find_taxa_with_primers(ps, primers)
  if (is.null(bad) || nrow(bad) == 0) {
    return(ps)
  }
  message("Removing ", nrow(bad), " taxa containing primer sequences (", sum(bad$n_reads), " reads).")
  phyloseq::prune_taxa(!phyloseq::taxa_names(ps) %in% bad$taxon, ps)
}

Code
#' Compare refseq sequences across three phyloseq objects
#'
#' Computes pairwise sequence similarity between the `@refseq` slots of three
#' phyloseq objects using k-mer Jaccard similarity, then groups near-identical
#' sequences via connected components (igraph). Produces a ggplot2 line plot
#' and a Venn diagram (ggVennDiagram) for each dissimilarity threshold.
#'
#' Similarity is approximated as `p ≈ (2j / (1 + j))^(1/k)` where `j` is the
#' Jaccard index of shared k-mer presence and `k` is the k-mer size. The
#' approximation is accurate within ~2 pp for identities between 90–100%.
#'
#' @param ps1,ps2,ps3 phyloseq objects whose `@refseq` slot will be compared.
#' @param labels Character vector of length 3. Display names for `ps1`, `ps2`,
#'   and `ps3` respectively. Default: `c("ps1", "ps2", "ps3")`.
#' @param thresholds Numeric vector of maximum dissimilarity thresholds (in %).
#'   `0` means exact matches only; `10` means sequences within 10% divergence
#'   are considered equivalent. Default: `c(0, 1, 2, 3, 5, 10)`.
#' @param k Integer. K-mer size used to build presence/absence profiles.
#'   Larger `k` increases specificity; smaller `k` increases sensitivity for
#'   divergent sequences. Default: `7`.
#'
#' @return A named list with three elements:
#'   \describe{
#'     \item{`line_plot`}{ggplot2 line plot: x = number of shared sequence
#'       clusters, y = minimum similarity threshold, one line per pairwise
#'       comparison.}
#'     \item{`venn_plots`}{Named list of ggVennDiagram objects, one per
#'       threshold. Names follow the pattern `"<threshold>_pct"`.}
#'     \item{`results`}{List of per-threshold results, each containing
#'       `threshold`, `venn_data`, `n12`, `n13`, and `n23` (number of shared
#'       sequence clusters between each pair).}
#'   }
compare_refseq_similarity <- function(ps1, ps2, ps3,
                                      labels = c("ps1", "ps2", "ps3"),
                                      thresholds = c(0, 1, 2, 3, 5, 10),
                                      k = 7) {
  seq1 <- as.character(ps1@refseq)
  seq2 <- as.character(ps2@refseq)
  seq3 <- as.character(ps3@refseq)
  n1 <- length(seq1)
  n2 <- length(seq2)
  n3 <- length(seq3)

  kmer_presence <- function(seqs) {
    dna <- Biostrings::DNAStringSet(seqs)
    (Biostrings::oligonucleotideFrequency(dna, width = k) > 0) * 1
  }

  jaccard_to_pid <- function(jaccard) {
    100 * (2 * jaccard / (1 + jaccard))^(1 / k)
  }

  compute_pid_matrix <- function(km_a, km_b, n_a, n_b) {
    shared <- tcrossprod(km_a, km_b)
    union_size <- outer(n_a, n_b, "+") - shared
    jaccard_to_pid(shared / pmax(union_size, 1))
  }

  message("Building k-mer profiles (k = ", k, ")...")
  km1 <- kmer_presence(seq1)
  km2 <- kmer_presence(seq2)
  km3 <- kmer_presence(seq3)
  n1k <- rowSums(km1)
  n2k <- rowSums(km2)
  n3k <- rowSums(km3)

  message("Computing ", labels[1], " vs ", labels[2], "...")
  pid12 <- compute_pid_matrix(km1, km2, n1k, n2k)
  message("Computing ", labels[1], " vs ", labels[3], "...")
  pid13 <- compute_pid_matrix(km1, km3, n1k, n3k)
  message("Computing ", labels[2], " vs ", labels[3], "...")
  pid23 <- compute_pid_matrix(km2, km3, n2k, n3k)

  process_threshold <- function(thresh) {
    if (thresh == 0) {
      all_seqs <- c(seq1, seq2, seq3)
      seq_groups <- split(seq_along(all_seqs), all_seqs)
      edges <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_groups, function(idx) {
        if (length(idx) < 2) {
          return(NULL)
        }
        t(combn(idx, 2))
      }))
    } else {
      min_pid_val <- 100 - thresh
      e12 <- which(pid12 >= min_pid_val, arr.ind = TRUE)
      e13 <- which(pid13 >= min_pid_val, arr.ind = TRUE)
      e23 <- which(pid23 >= min_pid_val, arr.ind = TRUE)
      if (nrow(e12) > 0) {
        e12[, 2] <- e12[, 2] + n1
      }
      if (nrow(e13) > 0) {
        e13[, 2] <- e13[, 2] + n1 + n2
      }
      if (nrow(e23) > 0) {
        e23[, 1] <- e23[, 1] + n1
        e23[, 2] <- e23[, 2] + n1 + n2
      }
      edges <- rbind(e12, e13, e23)
    }

    g <- igraph::make_empty_graph(n = n1 + n2 + n3, directed = FALSE)
    if (!is.null(edges) && nrow(edges) > 0) {
      g <- igraph::add_edges(g, t(edges))
    }
    comp_id <- igraph::components(g)$membership

    node_set <- rep(labels, c(n1, n2, n3))
    ct <- table(comp_id, node_set)
    in_A <- ct[, labels[1]] > 0
    in_B <- ct[, labels[2]] > 0
    in_C <- ct[, labels[3]] > 0
    comp_names <- paste0("c", rownames(ct))

    list(
      threshold = thresh,
      venn_data = stats::setNames(
        list(comp_names[in_A], comp_names[in_B], comp_names[in_C]),
        labels
      ),
      n12 = sum(in_A & in_B),
      n13 = sum(in_A & in_C),
      n23 = sum(in_B & in_C)
    )
  }

  results <- lapply(thresholds, process_threshold)

  df_line <- do.call(rbind, lapply(results, function(r) {
    data.frame(
      similarity = 100 - r$threshold,
      n_shared = c(r$n12, r$n13, r$n23),
      comparison = c(
        paste(labels[1], "vs", labels[2]),
        paste(labels[1], "vs", labels[3]),
        paste(labels[2], "vs", labels[3])
      )
    )
  }))

  p_line <- ggplot2::ggplot(
    df_line,
    ggplot2::aes(x = n_shared, y = similarity, color = comparison, group = comparison)
  ) +
    ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(size = 3) +
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(
      breaks = 100 - thresholds,
      labels = paste0(100 - thresholds, "%")
    ) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      x = "Number of shared sequence clusters",
      y = "Minimum sequence similarity",
      color = "Comparison"
    )

  venn_plots <- lapply(results, function(r) {
    thresh <- r$threshold
    ggVennDiagram::ggVennDiagram(r$venn_data, label_alpha = 0, label = "count") +
      ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#4CAF50") +
      ggplot2::labs(
        title = if (thresh == 0) "Exact matches" else sprintf("Within %d%% dissim.", thresh)
      ) +
      ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "none")
  })
  names(venn_plots) <- paste0(thresholds, "_pct")

  list(line_plot = p_line, venn_plots = venn_plots, results = results)
}

sim_results <- compare_refseq_similarity(
  d_16S, d_pmoA, d_mcrA,
  labels = c("16S", "pmoA", "mcrA"),
  thresholds = c(0)
)

# Build a long data frame with one row per (marker, sample, step).
# track_by_samples is a named list (one element per sample) of data frames
# with rownames = step names and columns nb_sequences / nb_clusters / nb_samples.
samp_to_long <- function(track_samp, marker) {
  bind_rows(lapply(names(track_samp), function(s) {
    data.frame(
      marker = marker,
      sample = s,
      rownames_to_column(track_samp[[s]], "step"),
      check.names = FALSE
    )
  }))
}

result_samp <- bind_rows(
  samp_to_long(track_16S_samp,  "16S"),
  samp_to_long(track_mcrA_samp, "mcrA"),
  samp_to_long(track_pmoA_samp, "pmoA")
) |>
  mutate(nb_clusters = ifelse(nb_sequences==0, 0, nb_clusters)) |> 
  mutate(
